nodes.name: 1. Webhook: Vendor Extract
nodes.notes: Receives POST with: imageBase64, mimeType, source (cookie_banner, screenshot, etc.)

parameters.text:
=Analyze this screenshot/image and extract all vendor, tracker, and third-party service names visible.

This is a {{ $json.source }} from a website. Look for:
1. Cookie consent dialogs - extract all listed vendors/trackers
2. Privacy banners - extract third-party services mentioned
3. Data sharing notices - extract partner companies
4. Any logos or company names visible

For each vendor found, provide:
- Name (exact as shown)
- Category (Analytics, Advertising, Social, Essential, Marketing, Other)
- Confidence (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)

Output as JSON:
{
  "vendors": [
    { "name": "Google Analytics", "category": "Analytics", "confidence": "HIGH" },
    { "name": "Facebook Pixel", "category": "Advertising", "confidence": "HIGH" }
  ],
  "totalFound": 2,
  "sourceType": "cookie_banner"
}

options.systemMessage: You are a privacy analyst specializing in identifying third-party trackers and data processors from website interfaces. Extract vendor names accurately.

nodes.name: 3. AI: OCR & Vendor Extraction
nodes.notes: Uses Gemini vision for OCR and vendor identification

parameters.query: INSERT INTO vendor_lists (source_type, website_url, vendors_json, total_count, extracted_at) VALUES ('{{ $json.source }}', '{{ $json.websiteUrl || '' }}', '{{ JSON.stringify($json.vendors).replace(/'/g, "''") }}', {{ $json.totalFound }}, NOW()) RETURNING id

nodes.name: 5. Save to Database
nodes.notes: Saves to vendor_lists table (will be created if doesn't exist)

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { success: true, vendors: $('4. Parse Vendor List').first().json.vendors, totalFound: $('4. Parse Vendor List').first().json.totalFound } }}

meta.description: Extracts vendor/tracker names from cookie consent screenshots using Gemini vision OCR. Saves to vendor_lists table for ONSIT discovery.
